global.title: Asia markets choppy as threat of Trump Hormuz levy spooks traders - Reuters
global.summary: Markets swung as renewed U.S.-Iran conflict disrupted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing oil higher and raising fears of inflation and further rate hikes.
global.whyItMatters: Energy shocks and war anxiety shape the emotional and economic climate people bring into church, offering context for prayer, care, and preaching.

global.title: Morning Bid: Fed in the spotlight as Warsh faces Congress - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters says investors are watching Fed testimony while also noting the Trump administration has blocked American citizens in the Democratic Republic of Congo from returning to the U.S. on commercial flights as the Ebola outbreak intensifies.
global.whyItMatters: This combines financial uncertainty with a growing public-health concern, both of which can heighten fear and instability in congregational life.

global.title: White House flags lowered after Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71 - Reuters
global.summary: The White House lowered flags after the death of Senator Lindsey Graham, a major figure in U.S. politics and foreign policy debates.
global.whyItMatters: The death of a prominent public leader can open pastoral space to reflect on legacy, mortality, and the stewardship of influence.

canadian.title: Canada to Provide Ukraine $900M for Ammo, Armoured Vehicles: Carney - Newser
canadian.summary: A Newser roundup notes Prime Minister Carney says Canada will provide $900 million in ammunition and armoured vehicles to Ukraine.
canadian.whyItMatters: This is a significant Canadian foreign-policy and spending decision that may shape how congregants think about war, peace, and national responsibility.

persecutedChurch.title: ICC official says breakthrough made in Darfur investigations - Reuters
persecutedChurch.summary: A Reuters report says the International Criminal Court has made a breakthrough in its Darfur investigations, where long-running violence has devastated vulnerable communities.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Justice developments in atrocity cases can guide informed prayer for the oppressed and remind the church that suffering communities must not be forgotten.

persecutedChurch.title: CAM weekly antisemitism report – "Destroy Israel" banner unfurled at Spanish festival - The Jerusalem Post
persecutedChurch.summary: The report tracks 111 antisemitic incidents globally this week, including a large 'Destroy Israel' banner displayed at Spain's San Fermín festival and harassment outside a Montreal synagogue.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Rising hostility toward Jewish communities is a pastoral concern for prayer, teaching against hatred, and vigilance about prejudice close to home.

leadership.title: The performance advantage: what fitness has taught me about leading a 24/7 business - Men's Health Magazine Australia
leadership.summary: The piece argues that sustainable leadership is built through habits, recovery, and consistency rather than constant intensity, with culture formed by what leaders repeatedly model.
leadership.whyItMatters: A pastor's pace teaches the church and staff how to live; this is a timely reminder that endurance and recovery are spiritual leadership issues too.

goodNews.title: Good News: Savannah Bananas and massive stadium audience wave to children’s hospital - NBC News
goodNews.summary: At Kinnick Stadium, the Savannah Bananas and a packed crowd joined the 'Hawkeye Wave,' turning a ballgame into a moment of encouragement for children watching from a nearby hospital.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a simple picture of public compassion—useful for reminding a congregation how small gestures can carry hope to people who are suffering.
